subjects.name: Numerical Methods and Transform Techniques

units.title: Iterative Methods
topics: Solutions of algebraic and transcendental equations: bisection, secant, false position and iteration methods
Newton-Raphson method (including simultaneous equations)
Interpolation: Newton's forward and backward formulae
Interpolation with unequal intervals; Lagrange's interpolation formula

units.title: Numerical Integration & Solution of ODEs
topics: Trapezoidal rule; Simpson's 1/3 and 3/8 rules
Solution of initial value problems by Taylor's series and Picard's method
Euler's method
Runge-Kutta method (second and fourth order); Milne's predictor-corrector method

units.title: Laplace Transforms
topics: Definition, Laplace transforms of standard functions, properties and shifting theorems
Transforms of derivatives and integrals; unit step and Dirac delta functions
Inverse Laplace transforms; convolution theorem (without proof)
Applications: solving ODEs (initial value problems) and integro-differential equations

units.title: Fourier Series
topics: Periodic functions; Fourier series of periodic functions
Dirichlet's conditions
Even and odd functions; change of interval
Half-range sine and cosine series

units.title: Fourier Transforms
topics: Fourier integral theorem (without proof); Fourier sine and cosine integrals
Infinite Fourier transforms; sine and cosine transforms
Properties and inverse transforms; convolution theorem (without proof)
Finite Fourier transforms

seo.title: Numerical Methods & Transform Techniques - MECH 2-1
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-1 Numerical Methods & Transform Techniques: iterative methods, numerical integration & ODEs, Laplace transforms, Fourier series and transforms.

subjects.notes: Common to all branches of Engineering (per the LBRCE course structure). Course code 23FE09, 3-0-0-3 credits and 5-unit syllabus read from the source PDF.

subjects.name: Thermodynamics

units.title: Basic Concepts & Zeroth Law
topics: System, boundary, surroundings, types of systems, macroscopic and microscopic viewpoints, control mass and volume, continuum
Properties, state, process (reversible and irreversible), cycle, quasi-static process, thermodynamic equilibrium
Path and point functions, specific heat, internal energy, enthalpy
Zeroth law of thermodynamics, measurement of temperature, constant volume gas thermometer

units.title: First Law of Thermodynamics
topics: First law for a closed system undergoing change of state and a cycle; p-v representation; forms of stored energy
Mechanical and non-mechanical work transfer; pdV and other work transfer
Open system: control volume, flow work, steady flow energy equation (SFEE)
Steady flow devices (nozzles, diffusers, turbine, compressors, throttling valves, heat exchangers); limitations of the first law; PMM-I

units.title: Second Law of Thermodynamics & Entropy
topics: Energy reservoirs, heat engine, refrigerator, heat pump; Kelvin-Planck and Clausius statements and their equivalence; PMM-II
Carnot cycle, Carnot theorem and corollary
Entropy, principle of entropy increase, T-s plot, Clausius inequality, entropy changes for ideal gases
Availability and irreversibility, Maxwell relations, third law of thermodynamics

units.title: Ideal Gas Mixtures & Pure Substances
topics: Equation of state, Avogadro's law, ideal/perfect/real gas; Dalton's and Amagat's laws
Internal energy, enthalpy, specific heats and entropy of gas mixtures
Pure substance: phases, p-v, p-T, T-s and h-s diagrams, p-v-T surface
Properties of steam, dryness fraction, phase change processes, Mollier chart

units.title: Thermodynamic Cycles
topics: Carnot vapour cycle; simple Rankine cycle and problems
Gas power cycles: Otto, Diesel, Dual and Brayton cycles
Refrigeration cycles: reversed Carnot and Bell-Coleman cycles
Working of the vapour compression refrigeration cycle

seo.title: Thermodynamics - JNTUK R23 MECH 2-1
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-1 Thermodynamics: basic concepts and Zeroth law, First and Second laws, entropy, ideal gas mixtures, pure substances and thermodynamic cycles.

subjects.notes: Professional Core. Course code 23ME03, 2-0-0-2 credits and 5-unit syllabus read from the source PDF.

subjects.name: Mechanics of Solids

units.title: Simple Stresses & Strains
topics: Elasticity and plasticity, types of stresses and strains, Hooke's law, stress-strain diagram for mild steel, working stress, factor of safety
Lateral strain, Poisson's ratio and volumetric strain; bars of varying section; composite bars; thermal stresses
Complex stresses; stresses on an inclined plane; principal planes and principal stresses; Mohr's circle; relation between elastic constants
Strain energy, resilience; gradual, sudden, impact and shock loadings

units.title: Shear Force and Bending Moment
topics: Definition and types of beams; concept of shear force and bending moment
SF and BM diagrams for cantilever, simply supported and overhanging beams
Beams subjected to point loads, UDL, uniformly varying loads and their combinations
Point of contraflexure; relation between SF, BM and rate of loading

units.title: Flexural Stresses & Torsion
topics: Theory of simple bending; derivation of bending equation; bending stresses
Section modulus of rectangular, circular, I and T sections; design of simple beam sections
Torsion of circular shafts; pure shear; transmission of power
Shafts in series and parallel

units.title: Shear Stresses & Deflection of Beams
topics: Shear stress distribution across rectangular, circular, triangular, I and T sections
Deflection of beams: slope, deflection and radius of curvature; differential equation of the elastic line
Double integration and Macaulay's methods for cantilever and simply supported beams
Mohr's theorem and moment area method

units.title: Thin & Thick Cylinders and Columns
topics: Thin cylindrical shells: longitudinal and circumferential stresses; hoop, longitudinal and volumetric strains; thin spherical shells
Wire-wound thin cylinders
Lame's equation; cylinders under inside and outside pressures; compound cylinders
Columns: buckling and stability; Euler's formula and its limitations; Rankine's formula

seo.title: Mechanics of Solids - JNTUK R23 MECH 2-1
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-1 Mechanics of Solids: simple stresses & strains, shear force & bending moment, flexural stresses, torsion, cylinders and columns.

subjects.name: Material Science and Metallurgy

units.title: Structure of Metals & Constitution of Alloys
topics: Crystallization of metals; atomic packing factor - SC, BCC, FCC and HCP
Grain and grain boundaries and their effect on properties; determination of grain size
Necessity of alloying; types of solid solutions
Hume-Rothery's rules; intermediate alloy phases and electron compounds

units.title: Equilibrium Diagrams
topics: Construction of equilibrium diagrams; isomorphous alloy systems; equilibrium cooling and heating; lever rule; coring
Eutectic systems, peritectic reaction
Solid-state transformations: allotropy, eutectoid and peritectoid reactions, phase rule
Binary phase diagrams: Cu-Ni, Bi-Cd and Fe-Fe3C

units.title: Ferrous & Non-ferrous Metals and Alloys
topics: Cast irons: white, malleable, grey, spheroidal graphite and alloy cast iron
Classification of steels; plain carbon and low alloy steels
Hadfield manganese steels; tool and die steels
Non-ferrous: structure and properties of copper and aluminium alloys

units.title: Mechanical Working & Heat Treatment
topics: Hot working, cold working, strain hardening, recovery, recrystallization and grain growth
Heat treatment of steels: annealing, normalizing, hardening
TTT diagrams, tempering, hardenability, Jominy end quench test
Surface-hardening methods; age hardening treatment

units.title: Ceramics & Advanced Materials
topics: Crystalline ceramics, glasses, cermets, abrasive materials
Classification of composites
Manufacturing methods of fiber and particle reinforced composites
PMC, MMC, CMC and CCCs

seo.title: Material Science & Metallurgy - R23 MECH 2-1
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-1 Material Science & Metallurgy: metal & alloy structure, equilibrium diagrams, ferrous/non-ferrous alloys, heat treatment and composites.

subjects.name: UHV 2 - Understanding Harmony and Ethical Human Conduct

units.title: Introduction to Value Education
topics: Right understanding, relationship and physical facility (holistic development and the role of education)
Understanding value education; self-exploration as the process
Continuous happiness and prosperity - the basic human aspirations; current scenario
Method to fulfil the basic human aspirations

units.title: Harmony in the Human Being
topics: Understanding the human being as the co-existence of the self and the body
Distinguishing between the needs of the self and the body; the body as an instrument of the self
Understanding harmony in the self; harmony of the self with the body
Programme to ensure self-regulation and health

units.title: Harmony in the Family and Society
topics: Harmony in the family - the basic unit of human interaction; 'Trust' - the foundational value
'Respect' - as the right evaluation; other feelings, justice in human-to-human relationship
Understanding harmony in the society
Vision for the universal human order

units.title: Harmony in the Nature/Existence
topics: Understanding harmony in nature
Interconnectedness, self-regulation and mutual fulfilment among the four orders of nature
Realizing existence as co-existence at all levels
The holistic perception of harmony in existence

units.title: Implications of the Holistic Understanding - Professional Ethics
topics: Natural acceptance of human values; definitiveness of (ethical) human conduct
A basis for humanistic education, humanistic constitution and universal human order
Competence in professional ethics
Holistic technologies and management models; strategies for transition towards value-based life and profession

seo.title: Universal Human Values 2 - R23 MECH 2-1
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-1 Universal Human Values 2: value education, harmony in the human being, family and society, harmony in nature and professional ethics.

subjects.name: Environmental Science

units.title: Multidisciplinary Nature & Natural Resources
topics: Definition, scope and importance of environmental studies; need for public awareness
Renewable and non-renewable resources; forest resources - use, over-exploitation, deforestation
Water resources - use and over-utilization, floods, drought, dams; mineral resources
Food resources - world food problems, effects of modern agriculture; energy resources

units.title: Ecosystems & Biodiversity
topics: Structure and function of an ecosystem; producers, consumers and decomposers; energy flow; ecological succession
Food chains, food webs and ecological pyramids; forest, grassland, desert and aquatic ecosystems
Biodiversity: genetic, species and ecosystem diversity; value of biodiversity; India as a mega-diversity nation
Threats to biodiversity; endangered and endemic species; in-situ and ex-situ conservation

units.title: Environmental Pollution & Solid Waste Management
topics: Air, water, soil, marine, noise and thermal pollution and nuclear hazards - causes, effects and control
Solid waste management: urban and industrial wastes
Role of an individual in prevention of pollution; pollution case studies
Disaster management: floods, earthquake, cyclone and landslides

units.title: Social Issues and the Environment
topics: From unsustainable to sustainable development; water conservation, rainwater harvesting, watershed management
Resettlement and rehabilitation; environmental ethics
Climate change, global warming, acid rain, ozone layer depletion; wasteland reclamation
Environment Protection Act, Air Act, Water Act, Wildlife and Forest Conservation Acts; public awareness

units.title: Human Population and the Environment
topics: Population growth and explosion; family welfare programmes
Environment and human health; human rights; value education
HIV/AIDS; women and child welfare; role of information technology
Field work: visit to local areas and polluted sites; study of common plants, insects and birds

seo.title: Environmental Science - JNTUK R23 MECH 2-1
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-1 Environmental Science (audit): natural resources, ecosystems, biodiversity, pollution, social issues and human population.

seo.title: Mechanics of Solids & Materials Science Lab - MECH 2-1

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Professional Core lab (0-0-3-1.5, course code 23ME53). Part A (mechanics of solids): tensile, bending, torsion, hardness (Brinell/Rockwell/Vickers), spring, impact (Charpy/Izod) and shear tests. Part B (material science): microstructure of pure metals, mild/medium/high carbon steels, cast irons, non-ferrous and heat-treated steels, Jominy end quench. Read from the source PDF.

subjects.notes: Skill-enhancement lab (0-0-2-1, course code 23CS57). Twelve experiments: Python setup and basics, functions and modules, lists and tuples, dictionaries and sets, strings and file I/O, error handling, OOP, libraries (NumPy/Pandas), data manipulation and visualization, web scraping and APIs, and databases (SQLite/SQLAlchemy). subjects.name: Embedded Systems & IoT

seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-1 Embedded Systems & IoT: microcontroller-transducer interfacing, serial communication with Arduino, and Raspberry Pi based IoT experiments.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Skill-enhancement course (0-1-2-2, course code 23ECS1). Given as two experiment groups rather than unit-wise theory. Embedded Systems: analog signal from temperature sensor, PWM output, HyperTerminal serial links, SPI DAC, stepper motor and accelerometer using Arduino. IoT: Raspberry Pi setup and Python IDE, distance sensor, public APIs/SDKs, ZigBee protocol, and sensor experiments on Arduino/Node MCU. Read from the source PDF.

subjects.name: Complex Variables, Probability and Statistics

units.title: Complex Variables & Complex Integration
topics: Continuity, differentiability, analyticity; Cauchy-Riemann equations in Cartesian and polar coordinates
Harmonic and conjugate harmonic functions; Milne-Thomson method
Complex integration: line integral, Cauchy's integral theorem
Cauchy's integral formula and generalized integral formula (without proofs)

units.title: Series Expansions & Residue Theorem
topics: Radius of convergence; Taylor's, Maclaurin's and Laurent series
Types of singularities: isolated, essential and pole of order m
Residues and residue theorem (without proof)
Evaluation of real integrals using the residue theorem

units.title: Probability and Distributions
topics: Review of probability and Baye's theorem; discrete and continuous random variables
Distribution functions: probability mass function, probability density function and cumulative distribution functions
Mathematical expectation and variance
Binomial, Poisson, Uniform and Normal distributions

units.title: Sampling Theory
topics: Population and samples; sampling distribution of means and variance
Central limit theorem (without proof); representation of normal theory distributions
Introduction to t, chi-square and F distributions
Point and interval estimations; maximum error of estimate

units.title: Tests of Hypothesis
topics: Hypothesis; null and alternative hypothesis; Type I and Type II errors
Level of significance; one-tail and two-tail tests
Tests concerning one mean and two means (large and small samples)
Tests on proportions

seo.title: Complex Variables, Probability & Statistics - MECH 2-2
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-2 Complex Variables, Probability & Statistics: complex integration, series & residues, distributions, sampling and hypothesis tests.

units.title: Casting
topics: Steps in making a casting; advantages and applications
Patterns and pattern making; types of patterns; pattern allowances; moulding and cores
Principles of gating, risers and casting design considerations
Special casting processes: centrifugal, die, investment and shell moulding

units.title: Welding
topics: Classification of welding processes; types of welded joints; gas welding, flames and oxy-acetylene cutting
Arc welding: MMAW, submerged arc, TIG and MIG, electro-slag welding
Resistance, friction, friction-stir, forge, explosive, thermit, plasma-arc, laser and electron-beam welding
Soldering and brazing; heat affected zones; pre- and post-heating

units.title: Bulk Forming
topics: Plastic deformation; recovery, recrystallization and grain growth; hot and cold working; strain hardening and annealing
Forging: types, defects and remedies

units.title: Sheet Metal Forming
topics: Blanking and piercing; forces and power requirements
Deep drawing, stretch forming, bending, spring back and remedies
Coining, spinning; types of presses and press tools

units.title: Powder Metallurgy
topics: Methods of producing metal powders: milling, atomization, granulation, reduction and electrolytic deposition
Compacting methods
Sintering and methods of manufacturing sintered parts
Secondary operations and applications of powder metallurgical products

subjects.name: Fluid Mechanics & Hydraulic Machines

units.title: Fluid Statics
topics: Properties of fluids: specific gravity, viscosity, surface tension, capillarity, vapor pressure
Atmospheric, gauge and vacuum pressure; measurement of pressure
Manometers: piezometer, U-tube, inverted and differential; Pascal's and hydrostatic laws
Buoyancy and flotation: metacenter, stability of floating and submerged bodies

units.title: Fluid Kinematics & Dynamics
topics: Flow types; continuity equation; circulation and vorticity; stream, path and streak lines; stream function and velocity potential
Euler's and Bernoulli's equations; momentum equation and force on a pipe bend
Closed conduit flow; Reynold's experiment; Darcy-Weisbach equation
Minor losses; pipes in series and parallel; hydraulic gradient and total energy lines

units.title: Boundary Layer Theory & Dimensional Analysis
topics: Momentum integral equation; displacement, momentum and energy thickness
Separation of boundary layer and control of flow separation; streamlined and bluff body
Dimensional analysis; dimensional homogeneity
Method of repeating variables and Buckingham Pi theorem

units.title: Turbo Machinery & Hydraulic Turbines
topics: Hydrodynamic force of jets on stationary and moving flat, inclined and curved vanes; velocity diagrams
Work done and efficiency; flow over radial vanes
Classification of turbines; impulse and reaction turbines
Pelton wheel, Francis and Kaplan turbines; draft tube theory, functions and efficiency

units.title: Performance of Turbines & Pumps
topics: Geometric similarity; unit and specific quantities; characteristic curves; governing; cavitation
Centrifugal pumps: working, work done, manometric head, losses and efficiencies, specific speed
Pumps in series and parallel; NPSH
Reciprocating pumps: working, discharge, slip, indicator diagrams

seo.title: Fluid Mechanics & Hydraulic Machines - MECH 2-2
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-2 Fluid Mechanics & Hydraulic Machines: fluid statics, kinematics & dynamics, boundary layer theory, hydraulic turbines and pumps.

units.title: Simple Mechanisms
topics: Classification of mechanisms; basic kinematic concepts; degree of freedom and mobility
Grashof's law; kinematic inversions of four-bar and slider-crank chains
Limit positions, mechanical advantage, transmission angle
Quick-return and straight-line mechanisms; universal joint; rocker mechanisms

units.title: Plane Motion Analysis
topics: Displacement, velocity and acceleration analysis of simple mechanisms
Graphical velocity analysis using instantaneous centers
Velocity and acceleration analysis using the relative velocity method
Coriolis component of acceleration

units.title: Gyroscope & Gear Profile
topics: Principle of gyroscope; gyroscopic effect in aeroplane, ship, car and two-wheeler

topics: Need for balancing; balancing of single and several masses in different planes (analytical and graphical)

units.title: Vibrations & Turning Moment Diagrams
topics: Free natural vibrations; Newton and energy methods for single degree of freedom
Damped vibrations (under, critical and over damped); forced vibrations; vibration isolation and transmissibility

Crank effort; coefficients of fluctuation of energy and speed; flywheel and its design

units.title: Design Thinking in Business Processes
topics: Design thinking applied in business and strategic innovation; principles that redefine business
Business challenges: growth, predictability, change, maintaining relevance, extreme competition, standardization
Design thinking to meet corporate needs; design thinking for startups
Defining and testing business models and cases; developing and testing prototypes

seo.title: Design Thinking & Innovation - R23 MECH 2-2
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-2 Design Thinking & Innovation: elements & principles of design, design thinking process, innovation, product design and business processes.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Course code 23ME57, 1-0-2-2 credits and 5-unit syllabus read from the source PDF.

subjects.name: Structural and Modal Analysis Using ANSYS

seo.title: Structural & Modal Analysis (ANSYS) - MECH 2-2
seo.meta_description: LBRCE R23 MECH 2-2 Structural & Modal Analysis Using ANSYS: FEM fundamentals, ANSYS Workbench, static structural analysis of beams and modal analysis.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Skill-enhancement course (0-1-2-2, course code 23MES1). Exercises using ANSYS Workbench: FEM introduction and ANSYS utilities; static structural analysis of stepped/tapered bars, planar truss, cantilever/simply-supported/fixed beams, flat plates and shells, axi-symmetric components; vibration analysis of spring-mass systems; modal analysis of beams, plates and shells. Read from the source PDF.
